examples: Line 9: Sylvanas-facing narration now uses 'the mage' instead of 'Jaina' to keep relational distance intact.
Lines 31-43: house-threshold beats keep the same clipped tactical voice without slipping into warmer naming.
Lines 69-99: granary passage now sounds like one mind throughout; the magic read stays spare and pressured.
Lines 105 and 135: repeated 'That would do' was split into 'Useful enough' and 'Enough' to avoid mechanical echo.
Lines 495-503: southbound exchange keeps the same dry voice and distance instead of softening into familiarity.
observations: Opening village scan matches the style target's short-to-medium tactical cadence.
Dialogue tags remain mostly invisible; the prose trusts the spoken lines.
Sylvanas's contempt still arrives through detail choice rather than editorial commentary.
Paragraph shape stays compact in the field-read sections and broadens only when the scene needs air.
The Paret scene now closes on a faint unease without over-signaling it.

examples: Line 95: 'The whole village answered the same way' now stays inside Sylvanas's immediate read rather than sounding like abstract narrator summary.
Line 185: Neral's rescue entrance is rendered as what Sylvanas sees, not as a detached camera note.
Line 337: Mira has 'seen enough of the road' instead of receiving a broader explanatory gloss.
Lines 427-447: Paret is judged by observable behavior—shock, counting, speed of answers—not by author verdict.
Line 479: the leftover sentence decoding Paret's farewell too explicitly was cut; the oddness remains an impression in Sylvanas's ear.
observations: No omniscient leak phrases appear in the chapter opening.
Mira's trauma remains external and sensory; the prose never claims interior access.
Jaina's magical reads are filtered through what she says or what Sylvanas notices physically.
The north-road deduction remains Sylvanas's tactical conclusion, not a narrator lecture.
The closing movement south keeps Sylvanas on the road rather than drifting into essay-like reflection.

examples: Lines 185-233: Neral is now consistently female through the cellar-rescue sequence.
Line 341: the comfort beat no longer misgenders Neral during the road-side aftermath.
Lines 361-377: dispatch and mounting beats now keep Neral's pronouns, mare, and body position consistent.
Lines 483-487: departure north now keeps Neral's cloak and warning tube aligned with the memory files.
The chapter still leaves the southbound party as Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel only.
notes: Millhaven remains a dead village with mobile dead headed north; no later-stage revelation was imported early.
The grain remains loudest, not deepest; the prose does not confirm it as the source.
Paret remains useful local intelligence, not exposed cultist or overt villain.
No organized-cult proof was added anywhere in the warning or dialogue.
Relationship state between Sylvanas and Jaina stays barbed and functional, with no early thaw.

examples: Line 25: 'Cold met her from inside: house-cold, a stove dead for hours' breaks a choppy run into a cleaner sentence wave.
Line 95: the village-summary sentence now carries one stronger pulse instead of three clipped fragments.
Lines 105 and 135: repeated cadence was broken by varying Sylvanas's brief judgments.
Line 335: 'moved north in a body:' gives the paragraph a longer breath before the geographic cascade.
Line 337: 'seen enough of the road' carries emotion forward in a fuller beat than the prior abrupt phrasing.
observations: The chapter still favors short pressure beats, which suits Sylvanas.
Long sentences are reserved for tactical accumulation or rolling inference.
Dialogue interruptions provide much of the chapter's syncopation.
The rescue sequence now alternates short commands with fuller descriptive sentences more cleanly.
The ending ride-out keeps the final cadence controlled instead of over-punctured.

examples: Granary finding still lands as 'loudest' rather than 'deepest,' preserving the planned evidence ceiling.
The warning is only authorized after churn, survivor testimony, road spoor, and Jaina's read converge.
Paret's geography still points first to Alder Run, then Three Willows, then the relay post, matching the warning logic.
The chapter closes southbound with the warning already sent north, preserving the plan's split-party handoff.
continuity.crossChapter: Chapter 3 opens in the same cold, suspicious tactical register carried out of chapter 2 and leaves that tension intact.
observations: No scene break markers were inserted across continuous movement beats.
The cellar rescue flows causally into questioning, then into the shed and road read.
Mira's testimony remains fragmented enough to feel traumatized rather than neatly exposited.
Paret's arrival interrupts the dispatch aftermath naturally rather than feeling pasted on.
The final exchange with Jaina acknowledges the proof threshold without turning it into reconciliation.

examples: Line 479 old version: the sentence parsing exactly why Paret's farewell sounded wrong was cut as over-explanation.
Repeated explicit naming of Jaina in Sylvanas-facing narration was trimmed across the chapter.
The rescue/comfort beats keep Mira's distress in bodily detail rather than abstract labeling.
No extra biography or world-exposition clauses were reintroduced during the polish.
observations: Scene openings begin with concrete disturbance, not weather or throat-clearing.
Parallel-structure fatigue in the early house search was reduced by sentence reshaping.
Explanatory afterthoughts were trimmed when the beat already landed without them.
The Paret scene now trusts silence and reaction more than annotation.
The final page stays sharp and spare without turning schematic.

examples: Jaina's explanatory lines stay plainspoken: 'loudest' versus 'deepest' instead of pseudo-scholarly terminology.
The field-warning banter keeps soldierly dryness rather than administrative diction.
Paret's speech remains rural and immediate; nothing in his mouth sounds like a report.
observations: Neral speaks with calm practical warmth, not therapeutic jargon.
Sylvanas's commands remain blunt and rank-shaped.
Jaina sounds educated but not modern-clinical.
Mira's speech stays broken and age-appropriate.
Paret's warning about the dead stays oral, vivid, and local.
